content.text:
Programming for Data Science
Semester:
1  |
Type:
UEM1  |
VHS:
42h  |
Weekly hours:
3h  |
Lecture:
1h30  |
Lab:
1h30
Personal work VHS:
21h  |
Coefficient:
2  |
Credits:
5
Teaching Objectives
Upon completion of this module, the student will be able to:
Master the basics of Python and good programming practices
Efficiently manipulate data structures for analysis
Use fundamental libraries such as NumPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Seaborn to explore and visualise data
Perform data processing, transformation, and preparation operations
Load, export, and clean datasets from various sources (CSV, JSON, Excel, SQL)
Prepare data for statistical analysis or machine learning algorithms
Course Content (21h)
I. Fundamental Python Concepts
Syntax and basic operations
Primitive data structures (integer, float, etc.)
Non-primitive data structures (set, dictionary, tuple, etc.)
Functions and functional programming with map, reduce, filter and lambda functions
Files, errors and exceptions
II. Data Manipulation with Pandas
Introduction to DataFrames, Series, Index Objects
Essential features: re-indexing, entry deletion, selection, filtering, sorting, aggregation
Descriptive analysis: statistics (correlation, variance), membership, unique values
Data transformation using functions
III. Arrays and Vectorial Computation
NumPy basics: vector creation, multidimensional array objects, data types for ndarrays, transposition, sorting, indexing
Arithmetic with NumPy arrays
Expressing conditional logic as a vectorised operation
IV. Loading Data, Storage, and File Formats
Reading data from various file formats (txt, csv, excel, json, ...)
Writing data to various file formats (csv, excel, json, ...)
Interaction with databases
V. Visualisation and Display Tools
Introduction to Matplotlib: Figure and subplot, legend, labels
Display with DataFrames and Seaborn (histogram, bar and line plot, etc.)
VI. Textual Data Manipulation
String object methods
Regular expressions
Vectorised string functions in Pandas
Vector representation of texts
VII. Object-Oriented Programming
Classes and objects
Inheritance and polymorphism
VIII. Packages and Modules
Hierarchical package structure, importing submodules using relative names
Module loading, splitting a module into multiple files
Code organisation and package installation
Assessment
Final examination (in person): 60%
Continuous assessment (CC): 40% — tests, personal work
Bibliography
Python Cookbook, Third Edition, Brian K. Jones, David M. Beazley, O'Reilly, 2011.
Fluent Python, Second Edition, Luciano Ramalho, O'Reilly, 2015.
Python by Example: Learning to Program in 150 Challenges, Nicola Lacey, Cambridge University, 2019.
Python for Data Analysis, Third Edition, Wes McKinney, O'Reilly, 2022.
Pandas for Everyone: Python Data Analysis, Second Edition, Daniel Y. Chen, Addison-Wesley Professional, 2017.
